label: Mild allergic rhinitis without wheeze or anaphylaxis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild allergic rhinitis.
No anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, wheeze/asthma flare, bacterial sinusitis concern, systemic illness, or specialist-directed allergy plan.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, throat/tongue swelling, wheeze, asthma/COPD exacerbation, respiratory distress, or hypoxia.
Bacterial sinusitis concern, severe facial pain, fever, orbital symptoms, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed allergy plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or allergy care if symptoms are not improving, recur often, or interfere with sleep or breathing.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports allergic-rhinitis/hay-fever framing, common nasal/eye symptoms, trigger reduction, clinician-recommended medicines, and escalation for severe allergy symptoms, non-improvement, or worsening wheeze/cough. Autonomous Phase 1701-1750 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed mild allergic rhinitis without anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, wheeze/asthma flare, bacterial sinusitis complication, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed allergy plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports allergic-rhinitis/hay-fever framing, common nasal/eye symptoms, trigger reduction, clinician-recommended medicines, and escalation for severe allergy symptoms, non-improvement, or worsening wheeze/cough. Autonomous Phase 1701-1750 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed mild allergic rhinitis without anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, wheeze/asthma flare, bacterial sinusitis complication, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed allergy plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild allergic rhinitis
no anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, wheeze, asthma flare, or trouble breathing documented
no bacterial sinusitis complication, systemic illness, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed allergy pathway
